entries.openingLines: If you really want to hear about it, the first thing you'll probably want to know is where I was born, and what my lousy childhood was like, and how my parents were occupied and all before they had me, and all that David Copperfield kind of crap, but I don't feel like going into it, if you want to know the truth.
entries.title: The Catcher in the Rye
entries.author: J.D. Salinger

entries.openingLines: I am an invisible man.
entries.title: Invisible Man
entries.author: Ralph Ellison

entries.openingLines: I first met Dean not long after my wife and I split up. I had just gotten over a serious illness that I won't bother to talk about, except that it had something to do with the miserably weary split-up and my feeling that everything was dead.
entries.title: On the Road
entries.author: Jack Kerouac

entries.openingLines: Okonkwo was well known throughout the nine villages and even beyond.
entries.title: Things Fall Apart
entries.author: Chinua Achebe

entries.openingLines: 124 was spiteful. Full of a baby's venom. The women in the house knew it and so did the children.
entries.title: Beloved
entries.author: Toni Morrison

entries.openingLines: I was thirty-seven then, strapped in my seat as the huge 747 plunged through dense cloud cover on approach to Hamburg airport.
entries.title: Norwegian Wood
entries.author: Haruki Murakami

entries.openingLines: For a man of his age, fifty-two, divorced, he has, to his mind, solved the problem of sex rather well.
entries.title: Disgrace
entries.author: J.M. Coetzee

entries.openingLines: I was born in the city of Bombay ..... once upon a time.
entries.title: Midnight's Children
entries.author: Salman Rushdie

entries.openingLines: The village of Holcomb stands on the high wheat plains of western Kansas, a lonesome area that other Kansans call "out there."
entries.title: In Cold Blood
entries.author: Truman Capote

entries.openingLines: We slept in what had once been the gymnasium.
entries.title: The Handmaid's Tale
entries.author: Margaret Atwood

entries.openingLines: I was the shadow of the waxwing slain / By the false azure in the windowpane
entries.title: Pale Fire

entries.openingLines: A screaming comes across the sky.
entries.title: Gravity's Rainbow
entries.author: Thomas Pynchon

entries.openingLines: In the beginning was the Word and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
entries.title: The Name of the Rose
entries.author: Umberto Eco

entries.openingLines: Here is the house. It is green and white. It has a red door. It is very pretty. Here is the family. Mother, Father, Dick, and Jane live in the green-and-white house. They are very happy.
entries.title: The Bluest Eye
entries.author: Toni Morrison

entries.openingLines: The play — for which Briony had designed the posters, programs, and tickets, constructed the sales booth out of a folding screen tipped on its side, and lined the collection box in red crêpe paper — was written by her in a two-week tempest of composition, causing her to miss a breakfast and a lunch.
entries.title: Atonement
entries.author: Ian McEwan

entries.openingLines: Early in the morning, late in the century, Cricklewood Broadway. At 6.27am on 1 January 1975, Alfred Archibald Jones was dressed in corduroy and sat in a fume-filled Cavalier Musketeer Estate face forward toward the most beautiful part of Cricklewood Broadway, which was not the Broadway itself but the little passage cut between Woolworths and Marks & Spencer through which, if you angled your head just right, you could see a small, cheerful quadrant of the Jubilee line station.
entries.title: White Teeth
entries.author: Zadie Smith

entries.openingLines: "'To be born again,' sang Gibreel Farishta tumbling from the heavens, 'first you have to die.'"
entries.title: The Satanic Verses
entries.author: Salman Rushdie

entries.openingLines: This is not for you.
entries.title: House of Leaves
entries.author: Mark Z. Danielewski

entries.openingLines: It seems increasingly likely that I really will undertake the expedition that has been preoccupying my imagination now for some days.
entries.title: The Remains of the Day
entries.author: Kazuo Ishiguro

entries.openingLines: Lolita, light of my life, fire of my loins. My sin, my soul. Lo-lee-ta: the tip of the tongue taking a trip of three steps down the palate to tap, at three, on the teeth. Lo. Lee. Ta.
entries.title: Lolita
entries.author: Vladimir Nabokov

entries.openingLines: Many years later, as he faced the firing squad, Colonel Aureliano Buendía was to remember that distant afternoon when his father took him to discover ice.
entries.title: One Hundred Years of Solitude
entries.author: Gabriel García Márquez

entries.openingLines: Mother died today. Or, maybe, yesterday; I can't be sure.
entries.title: The Stranger
entries.author: Albert Camus
